message:
test(stats): add dedicated JSON output tests for stats command

New test file test_stats_json.bats covers two distinct JSON output modes
of the stats command:

1. apr stats --json (legacy mode):
   - Tests behavior when metrics.json doesn't exist yet
   - Expects empty JSON object {} as output
   - Uses capture_streams to separate stdout from stderr
   - Validates JSON structure with jq

2. apr stats --export json (export mode):
   - Tests the structured export format
   - Validates schema_version field presence (should be "1.0.0")
   - Validates rounds array field presence
   - Uses assert_json_field_exists for validation

These tests complement the existing stats tests in test_commands.bats
by focusing specifically on JSON output correctness. The separation
allows for more focused testing of the JSON contract without mixing
in human-readable output tests.

Test setup:
- Requires jq (skips if not available)
- Creates test workflow with setup_test_workflow
- Uses standard setup/teardown from test_helper.bash

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,54 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bats
+# test_stats_json.bats - Integration tests for stats JSON output
+#
+# Tests: apr stats --json and --export json
+
+# Load test helpers
+load '../helpers/test_helper'
+
+# =============================================================================
+# Setup and Teardown
+# =============================================================================
+
+setup() {
+    setup_test_environment
+    log_test_start "${BATS_TEST_NAME}"
+
+    if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        skip "jq not available"
+    fi
+
+    cd "$TEST_PROJECT"
+    setup_test_workflow "default"
+}
+
+teardown() {
+    log_test_end "${BATS_TEST_NAME}" "$([[ ${status:-0} -eq 0 ]] && echo pass || echo fail)"
+    teardown_test_environment
+}
+
+# =============================================================================
+# Tests
+# =============================================================================
+
+@test "apr stats --json: outputs empty object when metrics missing" {
+    capture_streams "$APR_SCRIPT" stats --json
+
+    log_test_actual "stdout" "$CAPTURED_STDOUT"
+    log_test_actual "stderr" "$CAPTURED_STDERR"
+
+    assert_valid_json "$CAPTURED_STDOUT"
+    # Empty JSON object
+    [[ "$(echo "$CAPTURED_STDOUT" | jq -r 'keys | length')" -eq 0 ]]
+}
+
+@test "apr stats --export json: outputs metrics JSON" {
+    run "$APR_SCRIPT" stats --export json
+
+    log_test_output "$output"
+
+    assert_success
+    assert_valid_json "$output"
+    assert_json_field_exists "$output" ".schema_version"
+    assert_json_field_exists "$output" ".rounds"
+}
